checks.finding: T-001 (Risk: high, contracts/facet-manifest.schema.json — public API contract, INV-006 reverse dependency from Epic A3's check-component-coverage), T-002 (Risk: high, contracts/capability-summary.schema.json), and T-003/T-004 (Risk: high, context-projection.schema.json / compare-facet-manifest-staleness's REQ-004 Policy-Weakening fail-closed gate, security-spec.md B3) all correctly classify at high for a public-API-contract or fail-closed-gate sentinel surface per risk-classification-policy.md, each with a Risk Rationale citing the policy trigger directly, not self-certification. T-002's round-1 Major finding (self-certified medium for a new public API contract) is now fixed: Risk is high, Required Workflow: tdd, and its Risk Rationale (tasks.md ~line 379-397) explicitly cites the policy's 'public API contracts' trigger and quotes the retired self-certification language rather than repeating it. T-005 (Risk: medium) is verification/packaging work over already-authored, already-high-classified artifacts (vendored-copy --check extension + parity suite testing T-001..T-004's already-built scripts) — it does not itself create or modify a schema/auth/payment/PII/migration surface, and its own Risk Rationale reasons through, rather than asserts, this distinction — consistent with the medium tier.

T-003 (Risk: high, tasks.md line 714) and T-004 (Risk: high, tasks.md line 915) each end their Done When list with only 'An independent quality-gate verdict records PASS.' — this omits the 'a named reviewer distinct from the implementing agent' qualifier that sibling Risk: high tasks T-001 (tasks.md lines 339-340) and T-002 (tasks.md lines 520-522, the round-1 fix) both carry verbatim. A grep of the whole file for 'named reviewer'/'distinct from the implementing agent' confirms this qualifier appears only at T-001 and T-002, with no global definition elsewhere in tasks.md that would extend it to T-003/T-004 by reference. As written, T-003/T-004's Done When item does not commit the recorded verdict to a named reviewer distinct from the implementer, failing HIGH-CRITICAL-EVIDENCE's required item 'Independent review verdict recorded (a named second reviewer, not the implementing agent)' for two of the feature's four Risk: high tasks.

Each of T-001..T-005 has a Done When list of 5-7 checkable items (none exceeds 8) and represents one coherent artifact-family unit (schema authoring + its validator + its own test suite(s); one comparator script + its suite; or the cross-cutting parity/drift-gate suite over already-built T-001..T-004 artifacts). T-005 explicitly documents its own scope-breadth rationale ('Note on scope breadth (TASK-SIZE)', tasks.md ~line 1023) for bundling the vendored-copy check extension with the parity suite, citing the shared AC-032 fixture dependency between the two halves. No task's title implies sequential phasing, and no 3+ tasks are single-function/single-file fragments.

Every functional task (T-001..T-004) has extensive acceptance-tests.md negative/edge-case coverage beyond happy-path: T-001 — AC-002 (required-field-deletion matrix), AC-003/AC-006 (uniqueItems/enum rejection), AC-008 (upgrade_reasons-absent-now-rejected), AC-017/AC-018 (malformed/out-of-allowlist pointer rejection), AC-028/AC-047/AC-048 (five semantic-diagnostic negative fixtures); T-002 — AC-014 (additionalProperties rejection), AC-029 (non-zero-exit fixtures); T-003 — AC-030 (still-array-shaped components rejected), AC-042 (both/neither shared_paths branches rejected); T-004 — AC-023/AC-024/AC-040 (Block conditions), AC-044 (exit-3 argument-error class), AC-046 (inconsistent-tier rejection).

All TEST-NNN rows in acceptance-tests.md map 1:1 to a specific AC-NNN (acceptance-tests.md header: 'TEST IDs are namespaced to this feature... map 1:1 to requirements.md's Acceptance Criteria'), satisfying the acceptance-test scope requirement to reference a specific AC-NNN. T-005's facet-manifest-parity suite (AC-031/AC-032/AC-033/AC-043) spans all four scripts across three runtimes plus the vendored-copy drift gate, consistent with integration-test scope (multiple components); the five per-schema/per-script suites (T-001..T-004) each target one schema/script's own conformance, consistent with a narrower, single-artifact scope.

Each of T-001, T-002, T-003, T-004 (all Risk: high) carries an explicit Rollback: field describing git-revert of its two commits plus the human-copy re-staging/hand-revert procedure for the one protected surface (.github/workflows/test.yml) each stages. T-005 (medium, not required by this check) also carries one.

The only files multiple tasks write are the explicitly shared, serialized-append artifacts (tests/run-all.sh/.ps1, specs/epic-192-a4-facet-manifest/human-copy/.github/workflows/test.yml, MANIFEST.sha256, CHANGELOG.md), and tasks.md's own Global Constraints fixes a strict T-001→T-002→T-003→T-004→T-005 append order, enforced by each task's own Blockers field (T-002: T-001; T-003: T-001,T-002; T-004: T-001,T-002,T-003; T-005: T-001..T-004) — the allowed 'one task creates a file, a downstream task adds tests for it, provided the Blockers field enforces the ordering' exception applies. No two tasks list the same primary schema/script file.

Every Blockers relationship is explicitly justified in the task's own 'Depends On' text as either a genuine functional dependency (e.g. T-005 Depends On T-001..T-004: the parity suite invokes all four scripts against all fixtures and needs all three vendored schema copies to exist; T-004 Depends On T-001: the comparator invokes T-001's schema-conformance check on both manifest inputs before comparing) or an explicitly-labeled non-functional serialization dependency on the shared, order-sensitive tests/run-all.sh/test.yml append (T-002/T-003's Blockers on T-001; T-004's text states plainly 'T-002/T-003 are not functional dependencies'). No blocker lacks a stated rationale, and no task's scope depends on an undeclared predecessor's output.
SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope.

findings.finding: T-003 (tasks.md line 714) and T-004 (tasks.md line 915) each close their Done When list with 'An independent quality-gate verdict records PASS.' only — missing the 'a named reviewer distinct from the implementing agent' qualifier that sibling Risk: high tasks T-001 (lines 339-340) and T-002 (lines 520-522, the round-1 fix) both explicitly carry. No global definition elsewhere in tasks.md extends that qualifier to T-003/T-004 by reference (verified via full-file grep for 'named reviewer'/'distinct from the implementing agent'). This leaves two of the feature's four Risk: high tasks without a Done When item that commits the recorded verdict to a named, distinct-from-implementer reviewer, as HIGH-CRITICAL-EVIDENCE requires.
